title: Frozen banana stand opens on Balboa Island
description: The original family business — allegedly based on an idea taken from Korean partner Kim, a debt that resurfaces via Annyong decades later.

title: George Oscar Bluth II born
description: Firstborn son. The 'II' is load-bearing: he is not technically a Junior.

title: Michael Bluth born ('Nichael' on the birth certificate)
description: Second son; later paired with adopted 'twin' Lindsay, who is in fact three years older.

title: Lindsay (born Nellie Sitwell) adopted by the Bluths
description: Adopted primarily to spite rival developer Stan Sitwell; raised as Michael's twin. She learns the truth in 2006 (Family Ties, s03e11).

title: Byron 'Buster' Bluth born
description: Result of Lucille's affair with Oscar — 'just a turn of the head, and whoosh'; paternity is an open family secret.

title: Steve Holt born to Eve Holt
description: G.O.B.'s high-school son; the fact surfaces in 2005 (The Immaculate Election era).

title: Michael marries Tracey
description: George Michael arrives the following year.

title: George Michael Bluth born
description: Michael and Tracey's only child.

title: Mae 'Maeby' Fünke born
description: Conceived as part of a scheme of Lindsay and Tobias's that outlived its premise.

title: Tracey Bluth dies of ovarian cancer
description: Two years before the documentary begins; Michael raises George Michael alone.

title: George Sr. arrested by the SEC at his retirement party
description: Boat party on the ocean; charges include using the company as his personal piggy bank and, eventually, 'light treason' (homes for Saddam Hussein). The event that starts the documentary.

title: Michael takes over the Bluth Company
description: After being passed over for president in favor of Lucille, he stays to hold family and company together.

title: Banana stand burned down for the insurance
description: Michael and George Michael torch it, not knowing $250,000 cash lined the walls. There was always money in the banana stand.

title: Buster joins the army
description: Enlisted after a Michael Moore-lookalike ambush and Lucille's bluff-calling; serves mostly at a desk.

title: Buster's left hand bitten off by a loose seal
description: A seal wearing a yellow bow tie, freed by G.O.B. and given a taste for mammal blood. Ends Buster's army service; begins the hook era. Foreshadowed by the model-home loose-seal warnings and misheard 'Lucille' puns.

title: George Michael and Maeby's non-binding hospital 'wedding'
description: A vow-renewal stunt for a dying-seeming patient escalates; the cousins end up accidentally quasi-married. Later annulled in spirit if not paperwork.

title: Lucille steals the Queen Mary
description: Flees the second retirement party as the SEC closes in; runs aground. The charge that eventually sticks to her.

title: Lucille Austero disappears on Cinco de Cuatro
description: Vanishes from the harbor festivities after confronting several Bluths; blood on the staircar. The central mystery of s05; Buster is the chief suspect.

title: Lindsay runs for Congress
description: Takes over the seat race against (the missing) Lucille Austero, reversing her activist positions mid-rally.

title: George Michael launches 'Fakeblock' as George Maharis
description: A wood-block-sound app inflated by rumor into an anti-piracy privacy platform; the lie compounds until it collides with Michael and Rebel Alley.
